headline: Oil Prices Dive As Hormuz Opens; These S&P 500 Stocks Are 'Exhaling'
summary: Crude oil prices plunged more than $20 a barrel from Tuesday's peak after President Donald Trump agreed to a two-week ceasefire and Iran agreed to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.  United Airlines, Carnival and fellow cruise and airline stocks led the S&P 500 in morning trade.  Gold, silver and copper prices all jumped, fueling gains for Newmont and Freeport-McMoRan.

content.title: Oil Prices Dive As Hormuz Opens; These S&P 500 Stocks Are 'Exhaling'
content.summary: Crude oil prices plunged more than $20 a barrel from Tuesday's peak after President Donald Trump agreed to a two-week ceasefire and Iran agreed to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.  United Airlines, Carnival and fellow cruise and airline stocks led the S&P 500 in morning trade.  Gold, silver and copper prices all jumped, fueling gains for Newmont and Freeport-McMoRan.

headline: Iran ceasefire market moves: bitcoin prices surge, Exxon stock falls
summary: Market Catalysts host Julie Hyman and Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Brooke DiPalma take a look at some of Wednesday morning's trending tickers and stories.Bitcoin (BTC-USD) prices have surged to a three-week high on news of a ceasefire with Iran. The cryptocurrency is also rising after Iran requested that toll payments be made in crypto.Energy stocks, including Exxon (XOM), Chevron (CVX), and Occidental (OXY), are falling from the ceasefire news.Cruise stocks, including Norwegian (NCLH), Royal Caribbean (RCL), and Carnival (CCL), are soaring on eased US–Iran tensions.

content.title: Iran ceasefire market moves: bitcoin prices surge, Exxon stock falls
content.description:
Market Catalysts host Julie Hyman and Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Brooke DiPalma take a look at some of Wednesday morning's trending tickers and stories.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) prices have surged to a three-week high on news of a ceasefire with Iran. The cryptocurrency is also rising after Iran requested that toll payments be made in crypto.

Energy stocks, including Exxon (XOM), Chevron (CVX), and Occidental (OXY), are falling from the ceasefire news.

Cruise stocks, including Norwegian (NCLH), Royal Caribbean (RCL), and Carnival (CCL), are soaring on eased US–Iran tensions.
content.summary: Market Catalysts host Julie Hyman and Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Brooke DiPalma take a look at some of Wednesday morning's trending tickers and stories.Bitcoin (BTC-USD) prices have surged to a three-week high on news of a ceasefire with Iran. The cryptocurrency is also rising after Iran requested that toll payments be made in crypto.Energy stocks, including Exxon (XOM), Chevron (CVX), and Occidental (OXY), are falling from the ceasefire news.Cruise stocks, including Norwegian (NCLH), Royal Caribbean (RCL), and Carnival (CCL), are soaring on eased US–Iran tensions.

headline: Oil Prices Bounce; Iran Says Hormuz Opening Hinges On Israel, But S&P 500 Rallies
summary: Crude oil prices plunged more than $20 a barrel from Tuesday's peak after President Donald Trump agreed to a two-week ceasefire and Iran agreed to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.  United Airlines, Carnival and fellow cruise and airline stocks still ranked among top S&P 500 winners in morning trade.  Gold, silver and copper prices all jumped, fueling gains for Newmont and Freeport-McMoRan, though they came well off their highs.

content.title: Oil Prices Bounce; Iran Says Hormuz Opening Hinges On Israel, But S&P 500 Rallies
content.summary: Crude oil prices plunged more than $20 a barrel from Tuesday's peak after President Donald Trump agreed to a two-week ceasefire and Iran agreed to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.  United Airlines, Carnival and fellow cruise and airline stocks still ranked among top S&P 500 winners in morning trade.  Gold, silver and copper prices all jumped, fueling gains for Newmont and Freeport-McMoRan, though they came well off their highs.

headline: Is the Drop in Energy Stocks a Buying Opportunity? What Chevron’s Chart Says.
summary: Energy stocks are sliding today, but the pullback may be temporary.  The recently-announced cease-fire in the Middle East has eased immediate geopolitical concerns, removing the primary driver of higher crude prices.  Chevron  the ETF’s second-largest component at 17% of assets, looks attractive as it now trades 12% below its most recent 52-week high.

content.title: Is the Drop in Energy Stocks a Buying Opportunity? What Chevron’s Chart Says.
content.summary: Energy stocks are sliding today, but the pullback may be temporary.  The recently-announced cease-fire in the Middle East has eased immediate geopolitical concerns, removing the primary driver of higher crude prices.  Chevron  the ETF’s second-largest component at 17% of assets, looks attractive as it now trades 12% below its most recent 52-week high.
